deltas.releaseNoteText: New store type dedicated pages: array.mdx, map.mdx, text.mdx, xml.mdx — each with full setup, read/update/subscribe, version management, Yjs escape-hatch, and forceResetInitialContent patterns per type. The XML store in particular requires mutations via store.getXml() and Yjs APIs directly — update() must NOT be used.
deltas.summary: Create a new rule documenting the Array store type: useStore<T[]> with type:'array', createVeltStore with type:'array', Array.isArray() guard on value, store.getValue()/subscribe() for non-React, and forceResetInitialContent usage.

details.signature: useStore<Item[]>({ storeId: string, type: 'array', initialValue?: Item[], forceResetInitialContent?: boolean })

parameters.name: initialValue
parameters.description: Applied only when remote state is empty, unless forceResetInitialContent is true

parameters.name: forceResetInitialContent
parameters.description: If true, always resets store to initialValue on init

details.returnType: { value: T[] | null, update: (v: T[]) => void, store: Store, isLoading: boolean, isSynced: boolean, status: SyncStatus, error: Error | null }

deltas.releaseNoteText: New store type dedicated pages: array.mdx, map.mdx, text.mdx, xml.mdx — each with full setup, read/update/subscribe, version management, Yjs escape-hatch, and forceResetInitialContent patterns per type.
deltas.summary: Create a new rule documenting the Map store type: useStore<Record<string,any>> with type:'map', object-not-array guard on value, and key-level update patterns.

details.signature: useStore<DataMap>({ storeId: string, type: 'map', initialValue?: DataMap, forceResetInitialContent?: boolean })

parameters.name: initialValue
parameters.description: Applied only when remote state is empty

parameters.name: forceResetInitialContent
parameters.description: If true, always resets to initialValue on init

details.returnType: { value: DataMap | null, update: (v: DataMap) => void, store: Store, isLoading: boolean, isSynced: boolean, status: SyncStatus, error: Error | null }

deltas.releaseNoteText: New store type dedicated pages: array.mdx, map.mdx, text.mdx, xml.mdx — each with full setup, read/update/subscribe, version management, Yjs escape-hatch, and forceResetInitialContent patterns per type.
deltas.summary: Create a new rule documenting the Text store type: useStore<string> with type:'text', textarea binding pattern, store.getValue()/subscribe() for non-React.

details.signature: useStore<string>({ storeId: string, type: 'text', initialValue?: string, forceResetInitialContent?: boolean })

parameters.name: initialValue
parameters.description: Applied only when remote state is empty

parameters.name: forceResetInitialContent
parameters.description: If true, always resets to initialValue on init

details.returnType: { value: string | null, update: (v: string) => void, store: Store, isLoading: boolean, isSynced: boolean, status: SyncStatus, error: Error | null }

deltas.releaseNoteText: New XML store page: xml.mdx. Unlike text/map/array stores, the XML store does NOT use update(). All mutations must go through Yjs APIs directly via store.getXml(). Requires yjs package. Uses store.getDoc().transact() for batching.
deltas.summary: Create a new rule documenting the XML store type with its critical constraint: update() must NOT be called — all mutations go through store.getXml() (Y.XmlFragment) and Yjs APIs. The xml type requires yjs as an additional direct dependency.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Yjs Y.XmlFragment backend — mutations via Yjs only

details.returnType: { store: Store, isLoading: boolean, error: Error | null }

deltas.releaseNoteText: REST API Services: 17 services are included: organizations, folders, documents, users, userGroups, notifications, commentAnnotations, activities, accessControl, crdt, presence, livestate, recordings, rewriter, gdpr, workspace, and token.
deltas.summary: Add sdk.api.rewriter (and sdk.api.userGroups if missing) to the service table in the Python REST API backend rule. The sdk.api.rewriter namespace provides server-side access to the Rewriter API.
